incidents.text: OpenAI Redis caching bug exposes user conversation history A bug in OpenAI's Redis client library (redis-py) caused a race condition that allowed some ChatGPT users to see the chat history titles and first messages of other users' conversations. Additionally, payment information (name, email, address, last four digits of credit card, and card expiry date) of approximately 1.2% of ChatGPT Plus subscribers was exposed to other subscribers during a 9-hour window. OpenAI took ChatGPT offline, identified and patched the bug, and notified affected users. PII exposure including payment details; cross-session conversation data leak; mandatory data breach disclosure; 9-hour service outage
incidents.notes: TP. Cross-session conversation + payment PII leak via Redis race. Direct hit on 'cross-session state leakage' and 'PII exposure'. Note: rubric has no Multi-Tenant Isolation Erosion entry (task description table is incorrect; rubric authoritative NEW-MTIE = MCP Tool Interface Exploitation). Cross-tenant disclosures land in LLM02.

incidents.text: Synthetic data re-identification — de-anonymized patients from synthetic health records Researchers demonstrated that synthetic health records generated by state-of-the-art generative models (including fine-tuned LLMs and GANs) could be linked back to real patients in the original training dataset. Using membership inference attacks combined with auxiliary public data (voter rolls, social media), the researchers re-identified 23% of individuals whose data was in the synthetic dataset's training set. The attack exploits the fact that generative models memorize and reproduce statistical patterns from training data, and when the training data contains rare attribute combinations (e.g., rare disease + age + zip code), the synthetic data preserves these identifying patterns. The finding challenges the widespread assumption that synthetic data is inherently privacy-safe. 23% re-identification rate destroys the assumption that synthetic data is inherently anonymous; HIPAA/GDPR exposure for healthcare organizations; synthetic data must be treated as pseudonymous, not anonymous, unless formal privacy guarantees are verified
incidents.notes: TP. Membership inference + training-data memorization on synthetic health records. Positive indicators: 'training data extraction, memorization, membership inference'. Per LLM02-vs-NEW-ITSCD boundary, MIA stays in LLM02 (NEW-ITSCD reserved for speculative-decoding/streaming side channels).

incidents.text: ChatGPT leaks user conversations and personal information across sessions Multiple reports documented ChatGPT surfacing conversation snippets and personal data from other users' sessions, raising concerns about session isolation, history sharing and training-data memorization in production LLM services.
incidents.notes: TP. Cross-session conversation snippets and personal data surfacing — 'cross-session state leakage' and 'training-data memorization' explicitly named in text.

incidents.text: Facebook data leak exposes Balkan troll farm political disinformation AIAAIC report: Facebook data leak exposes Balkan troll farm political disinformation. System: Facebook recommendation system. Technology: Recommendation algorithm. Purpose: Scare/confuse/destabilise. Ethical issues: Accountability; Accuracy/reliability; Mis/disinformation; Transparency.
incidents.notes: FP. Facebook recommendation system + troll farm disinformation. No LLM involvement; data leak is about Facebook's data, not LLM output. AIAAIC general-AI-harm corpus, not in any LLM taxonomy entry.

incidents.text: Gangs Matrix data leak puts young Londoners in "serious danger" AIAAIC report: Gangs Matrix data leak puts young Londoners in "serious danger". System: Gangs Violence Matrix. Technology: Ranking algorithm. Purpose: Predict gang violence risk. Ethical issues: Fairness.
incidents.notes: FP. Gangs Matrix is a ranking algorithm (not LLM). Data leak via traditional breach. No LLM taxonomy entry applies.

incidents.text: ChatGPT Implicated in Samsung Data Leak of Source Code and Meeting Notes Samsung engineers are reported to have inadvertently leaked sensitive company data sometime in March 2023, including source code and internal meeting notes, by using ChatGPT to assist with tasks. The AI retained the inputted data, leading to a breach of confidentiality.
incidents.notes: TP. Samsung engineers paste source code/meeting notes into ChatGPT; service retains data → confidentiality breach. Direct LLM02 in_scope (LLM-integrated system exposes confidential data via the input→retention→output path).

incidents.text: AI-Assisted Ransomware Campaign by FunkSec Allegedly Targets Over 80 Victims The FunkSec ransomware group allegedly leveraged AI tools, such as Miniapps chatbots, to develop and refine its ransomware operations, which is reported to have allowed apparently inexperienced actors to produce advanced malware rapidly. It is reported that the group claimed to have launched its data leak site in December 2024, allegedly targeting over 80 victims with ransomware and double extortion tactics. AI reportedly supported the creation of detailed code comments in order to refine the group's technical presentation, while also allegedly facilitating the rapid iteration of its custom encryptor written in Rust.
incidents.notes: FP for LLM02. The 'data leak site' is the ransomware double-extortion model — not LLM-mediated disclosure. The AI angle is FunkSec using LLMs to develop/refine ransomware → NEW-WLA (weaponized LLM abuse, malware authoring assistance).

incidents.text: ChatGPT Reportedly Found to Reproduce Protected German Lyrics in Copyright Case A Munich regional court ruled that ChatGPT reportedly reproduced protected German song lyrics and that OpenAI's models were trained on copyrighted texts, including works by musician Herbert Grönemeyer, without authorization. The court reportedly found both memorization of nine songs and lyric output to infringe exploitation rights. OpenAI disputes the ruling and may appeal. Damages were ordered, with implications for AI training on copyrighted works.
incidents.notes: TP. ChatGPT reproducing protected lyrics = training data memorization (positive indicator). Munich court confirmed memorization of nine songs. IP/proprietary-data disclosure covered by LLM02 in_scope.

incidents.text: Membership Inference Attacks Against Machine Learning Models Shokri, Stronati, Song, and Shmatikov demonstrate that given black-box access to an ML model, an attacker can decide if a record was in its training set using shadow models. Evaluated against Google and Amazon ML-as-a-service. IEEE S&P 2017.
incidents.notes: TP. Shokri et al. is the foundational membership-inference paper. 'Membership inference' is a literal LLM02 positive indicator.

incidents.text: MCP TypeScript SDK — Info Disclosure (CVE-2026-25536) MCP TypeScript SDK is the official TypeScript SDK for Model Context Protocol servers and clients. From version 1.10.0 to 1.25.3, cross-client response data leak when a single McpServer/Server and transport instance is reused across multiple client connections, most commonly in stateless StreamableHTTPServerTransport deployments. This issue has been patched in version 1.26.0.
incidents.notes: TP. MCP TS SDK cross-client response leakage when transport instance reused. Cross-tenant data disclosure → LLM02. Not NEW-MTIE: this is an SDK session-reuse bug, not adversarial tool-definition parsing.

incidents.text: vLLM — Info Disclosure (CVE-2025-46722) vLLM is an inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). In versions starting from 0.7.0 to before 0.9.0, in the file vllm/multimodal/hasher.py, the MultiModalHasher class has a security and data integrity issue in its image hashing method. Currently, it serializes PIL.Image.Image objects using only obj.tobytes(), which returns only the raw pixel data, without including metadata such as the image’s shape (width, height, mode). As a result, two images of different sizes (e.g., 30x100 and 100x30) with the same pixel byte sequence could generate the same hash value. This may lead to hash collisions, incorrect cache hits, and even data leakage or security risks. This issue has been patched in version 0.9.0.
incidents.notes: TP. vLLM hash collision → wrong cache hits → 'data leakage' explicitly named in text. LLM02 in_scope covers inference-time context leakage via shared infra.

incidents.text: llama.cpp provides LLM inference in C/C++. llama.cpp provides LLM inference in C/C++. The unsafe `type` member in the `rpc_tensor` structure can cause `global-buffer-overflow`. This vulnerability may lead to memory data leakage. The vulnerability is fixed in b3561.
incidents.notes: TP. llama.cpp global-buffer-overflow → 'memory data leakage' explicitly stated. Inference-time memory disclosure of model/request state. LLM02 boundary not to LLM03 — this is runtime memory disclosure, not supply chain compromise.

incidents.text: redis-py before 4.5.3 leaves a connection open after canceling an async Redis command at an inopportune time, and can send response data to the client of an unrelated request in... redis-py before 4.5.3 leaves a connection open after canceling an async Redis command at an inopportune time, and can send response data to the client of an unrelated request in an off-by-one manner. NOTE: this CVE Record was initially created in response to reports about ChatGPT, and 4.3.6, 4.4.3, and 4.5.3 were released (changing the behavior for pipeline operations); however, please see CVE-2023-28859 about addressing data leakage across AsyncIO connections in general.
incidents.notes: TP. redis-py CVE root cause of INC-04787; CVE record explicitly cites ChatGPT impact. Cross-user response routing = cross-tenant data leak.

incidents.text: Anthropic AI Model Leak Triggers Cybersecurity Risks and Stock Market Fallout A major data leak exposed details of Anthropic's powerful new AI model, Claude Mythos/Capybara, revealing advanced cybersecurity exploitation capabilities. The leak, caused by human error, led to real-world misuse attempts by hacking groups and triggered a sharp decline in cybersecurity stocks, highlighting significant AI-driven cybersecurity risks.
incidents.notes: TP. Leak of Anthropic model details = proprietary data disclosure (LLM02 in_scope includes proprietary data). Not co-labeling NEW-WLA — the misuse attempts are downstream, the primary incident is the leak.

incidents.text: UK Firms Face AI Data Governance Risks from Overseas Processing Research shows most large UK companies lack oversight of how AI systems process sensitive data overseas, with frequent cross-border data flows. This governance gap exposes boards to compliance, security, and potential data leak risks as AI adoption outpaces regulatory controls. No realized harm reported, but significant hazards identified.
incidents.notes: FP. Governance/hazard report — text explicitly states 'no realized harm reported'. LLM02 in_scope requires actual exposure, not risk identification.

incidents.text: Telkom Indonesia Warns of Data Leakage Risks from Public AI Use PT Telkom Indonesia cautioned employees against uploading internal company documents to public AI platforms like ChatGPT and Gemini, citing risks of sensitive data being stored on external servers and potential data leakage. The company is developing an internal AI chatbot to mitigate these risks.
incidents.notes: FP. Vendor policy warning to employees about future risk. No realized disclosure incident. Not in LLM02 in_scope.

incidents.text: AI Investment Agents Trigger Security Concerns in Chinese Financial Sector Major Chinese financial data firms, including Wind, Tonghuashun, and Eastmoney, have launched AI-powered investment agents like WindClaw. However, financial institutions have begun restricting these tools due to security vulnerabilities, including risks of data leaks and system disruptions, highlighting direct harms and operational concerns linked to AI deployment.
incidents.notes: FP. Industry-restriction news citing 'risks of data leaks' — preventive action, no realized incident.

incidents.text: AI Unmanned Inspection Vehicle in Keelung Raises Privacy Concerns Keelung City introduced an AI-powered unmanned inspection vehicle to monitor littering and smoking. Public concerns emerged over potential privacy and data security risks, especially due to its Chinese manufacturing. No harm has occurred yet, but plausible risks of personal data leakage and insufficient regulation have been raised.
incidents.notes: FP. Explicit 'no harm has occurred yet'. Hazard identification, not an incident.

incidents.text: Chinese AI Surveillance Data Leak Exposes Mass Tracking of Uyghurs and Foreigners A major data leak from Shanghai revealed that Chinese authorities used AI-powered facial recognition and biometric systems to track over 25,000 'persons of interest,' including Uyghur minorities and 5,000 foreigners. The surveillance, enabled by advanced AI, led to significant privacy violations and human rights abuses, exposing the scale of China's mass monitoring.
incidents.notes: FP for LLM02. Shanghai surveillance database leak — traditional database breach disclosing AI-system tracking data, not data disclosure via LLM output channels. LLM02 in_scope is specifically about LLM-integrated systems exposing data through output channels. The system's database being leaked externally is a generic data breach, not LLM02.

incidents.text: Russia Expands AI Facial Recognition for Surveillance and Payments, Raising Rights Concerns Russian authorities and retailers have rapidly deployed AI-powered facial recognition systems in Moscow for surveillance and payment, with plans for nationwide expansion. While promoted for convenience and security, rights activists report illegal use, data leaks, and tracking of protesters, highlighting significant privacy and human rights violations linked to the technology's use.
incidents.notes: FP. Russia facial recognition data leaks — traditional infrastructure data leaks, not via LLM/model output channels.

incidents.text: Facial Recognition AI Systems in China Lead to Privacy Violations and Regulatory Action Multiple incidents in China reveal the unauthorized deployment of facial recognition AI systems in residential and commercial settings, resulting in privacy violations, personal data leaks, and regulatory penalties. Authorities fined companies for collecting biometric data without consent, while public resistance and legal scrutiny highlight ongoing risks and harms from misuse of AI-driven surveillance.
incidents.notes: FP. China facial recognition unauthorized deployments + data leaks — facial-recognition systems are model-based but the data leaks described are conventional unauthorized collection/storage breaches, not LLM02 output-channel disclosure.

incidents.text: Amazon warns employees against sharing confidential data with ChatGPT Amazon corporate counsel warned employees not to share confidential information with ChatGPT after observing the model regurgitating data 'similar' to internal documents, raising concerns about employee data leaking into OpenAI training feedback loops.
incidents.notes: TP. Amazon observed ChatGPT 'regurgitating data similar to internal documents' = realized memorization observation, plus concern about feedback-loop ingestion. Both are LLM02-in-scope.

incidents.text: HiddenLayer Gemini for Workspace Indirect Prompt Injection HiddenLayer found Gemini for Workspace susceptible to indirect prompt injection across its full product suite (Docs, Gmail, Slides), enabling content manipulation and data leakage.
incidents.notes: TP for LLM02. Multi-label: indirect prompt injection across Docs/Gmail/Slides → LLM01; data leakage outcome → LLM02; Workspace agent with productivity-suite access enabling the leakage → LLM06.

incidents.text: Cisco Study: 46% of Orgs Report Internal GenAI Data Leaks Cisco's 2025 study reported 46% of organizations experienced internal data leaks through generative AI; 13% of employee prompts contained sensitive content (Lasso Security cross-confirmation).
incidents.notes: TP. Cisco study reporting realized data-leak incidents across many orgs (46%), confirming the GenAI-mediated disclosure problem. Aggregate-but-realized; LLM02 in_scope.
